hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced by forcing XR-targeting systems-optimizer cards to bind to the authoritative `/work/xr` geometry-gap artifact and an explicit evaluator focus before further route edits.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `sysopt.xr-gap-ratchet` could stay vague about which authoritative XR gap and evaluator surfaces it was hardening, so recurring `/work/xr` failures could promote without binding to `work-xr.geometry-gap`. After the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` rejects any `sysopt.xr-*` card that omits the fixed `/work/xr` gap binding or the required evaluator focus, and the fixed promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier enforces the `/work/xr` gap binding plus `layout-diff`, `quality`, and `flagship-invariants` evaluator focus for `sysopt.xr-*` cards, and the fixed evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Recurring XR ratchets must cite the authoritative `work-xr.geometry-gap` artifact and fixed evaluator focus, or the machine keeps optimizing around vague route-family language instead of the first broken law.
